# Source Card: Integrated proteogenomic analysis for inherited bone marrow failure syndrome

## Summary
According to PubMed, this primary Leukemia 2024 study (Wakamatsu et al., [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41375-024-02263-1), PMID 38740980) applies mass-spectrometry proteomics to a pediatric-predominant Japanese inherited bone-marrow-failure-syndrome (IBMFS) registry, establishing that reduced SBDS and ADH5 protein expression can diagnose/screen Shwachman-Diamond syndrome (SDS) and ADH5/ALDH2 deficiency where DNA sequencing and standard clinical screening tests fall short. It reports a targeted-proteomic screening assay (SBDS/ADH5/WASP normalized to GAPDH) with defined mean-2SD cutoffs and sensitivity/specificity on 417 samples. For the pediatric CDS module it supplies research-grade assay-performance figures and a small set of clinical trigger thresholds (age of onset, telomere SD), all method- and cohort-specific rather than portable clinical reference intervals.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) SBDS screen 85.7% sens / 93.4% spec; ADH5 screen 100.0% sens / 97.5% spec on 417 samples — locator: Results, rapid-diagnostic-system final paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:24d561ee102e0c7b1b4559d312782bb7412a5547cec44cad1d205e0400e7d1b8]"
- (ev_002) Cutoff = cohort mean minus two standard deviations (numeric magnitude not reliably transcribable) — locator: Results, same paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a19534a3ad7f5c3126fe93220b56484b99e4576f827158e069eff600db09df1c]"
- (ev_003) 6/6 SDS had decreased SBDS protein; 2 not diagnosed by DNA-seq alone — locator: Abstract / Results diagnosis paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b146519f4a7e405f4e8b57cd91c84849f04250bbf89b794b9589d179bcc717a8]"
- (ev_004) Discovery cohort n=60, 27 male, median age at onset 3 y (range 0-23) — locator: Results, clinical/genetic diagnoses paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a32fd138177306c0c416d35d438b05adbf62fe3dbe4a6d018d792e831ff54d3c]"
- (ev_005) IBMFS-NOS working criteria: early onset <2 years, telomere length <2.0 SD — locator: Methods, Patients and samples — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c817896259a2c1a4753553e3dba15bc419fcf3faabdba0165c2e421acc3afc79]"
- (ev_006) 15-30% of SDS progress to MDS/AML — locator: Introduction para 2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:62c8770197c1b736135a66735a292ff421995ae6aa539ef87843932a35e6338e]"
- (ev_007) ALDH2 rs671 present in 25.5% of East Asians — locator: Introduction para 3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:9a776c1883ad4fa42179c11ae1fc80227affdf6243d1078267484b8ac99d337d]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: pediatric-predominant Japanese IBMFS registry; discovery cohort n=60 (median onset 3 y, range 0-23 y, 27 male), expansion/targeted cohort covering 417 samples (390 IBMFS-related disorders + 27 healthy controls). Includes young adults, so not exclusively pediatric.
- Assay: all protein thresholds are mass-spectrometry (DIA-MS discovery; SureQuant DDA-MS targeted), with SBDS/ADH5/WASP levels normalized to GAPDH protein. These are analyzer- and method-dependent research ratios, NOT harmonized clinical reference intervals; cutoffs (mean-2SD) are cohort-derived. Any CDS use is an implementation proposal requiring lab-specific re-derivation.
- Lifecycle: published 2024-05 (Leukemia vol 38 issue 6, pp 1256-1265); no retirement/guideline-version supersession stated.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Referral/escalation relevance: the IBMFS-NOS criteria (age of onset <2 y, telomere length <2.0 SD, chromosome-breakage hypersensitivity) function as suspicion/escalation triggers for further IBMFS work-up. Reduced SBDS or ADH5 protein flags SDS / ADH5-ALDH2 deficiency for genetic confirmation and cancer-surveillance pathways. This card records these as evidence for a CDS converter; it prescribes no diagnosis, treatment, or transplant decision.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP: the absolute SBDS and ADH5 cutoff magnitudes ("1.5 x 10^? and 9.0 x 10^?" GAPDH-normalized units) are NOT locatable — the scientific-notation exponents and units were stripped in the retrieved full text, so the numeric cutoff value is deliberately NOT asserted (only the mean-2SD comparator rule, ev_002, is quotable). Verify against the published PDF/Methods before any implementation.
- Small per-disorder discovery numbers (SDS n=6, ADH5/ALDH2 deficiency n=4); the authors state diagnostic tests for IBMFS other than SDS and ADH5/ALDH2 deficiency could not be established.
- Assay thresholds are single-lab MS ratios normalized to GAPDH; protein-vs-mRNA correlation across multicenter stored specimens was low (mean Spearman r 0.114), and the authors call for large-scale fresh-specimen studies before a reproducible clinical test.
- ADH5/ALDH2 deficiency and rs671 skew to East Asian populations; ADH5 protein screening should be paired with ALDH2 genotyping (ev_007) — a population-specific caveat, not a conflict with other sources.
- No conflicting numeric thresholds from other sources recorded here; conflicts, if any, are to be preserved by the CDS converter, not averaged.
